questions.question: A man walks 5 km North, then 3 km East. How far is he from his starting point?
Starting from a point, Ravi walks 2 km South, then 4 km West, then 4 km North. In which direction is he from his starting point?
questions.explanation: Displacement: 2N-2S=0 net N-S? Walk 2S, 4W, 4N → net position: 2N, 4W = North-West.

questions.question: A person faces North. He turns 90° clockwise. Which direction does he face?
Ram goes 10 km East, then 6 km North, then 5 km West. How far is he from start?
questions.explanation: Net East=10-5=5km, Net North=6km. Distance=√(25+36)=√61 km.

questions.question: If South-West is North, then what is West?
questions.explanation: If SW→N, rotating 135° clockwise: N→E, E→SE, SE→S... so W → NE.

questions.question: A man faces West. He turns 45° clockwise twice. Which direction does he now face?
questions.explanation: West + 45°×2 = West + 90° clockwise. West + 90° CW = North.

questions.question: Starting from a point, Amit walks 7 km North, turns left and walks 3 km, then turns right and walks 5 km. How far is he from start and in which direction?
questions.explanation: Goes 7N, 3W, 5N → total 12 km North and 3 km West. Distance from start = √(12²+3²)=√153. Direction=North-West.
